perturbation.original_text: This Agreement and the rights and obligations of the parties hereunder shall be governed by the material laws of the Federal Republic of Germany.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement and the rights and obligations of the parties hereunder shall be governed by the laws of Munich.
perturbation.explanation: By specifying 'the laws of Munich' instead of the 'Federal Republic of Germany', the contract attempts to bypass mandatory federal consumer protection laws that would otherwise apply. This is a legal contradiction as it tries to narrow the applicable law to a local level, potentially excluding broader consumer rights.
perturbation.law_explanation: § 307 BGB (German Civil Code) protects consumers from unreasonable contractual terms. By limiting the governing law to 'Munich,' the contract could be interpreted as an attempt to avoid the broader consumer protection provisions of the BGB, which apply throughout Germany.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 307 BGB - Einzelnorm zurück weiter Nichtamtliches Inhaltsverzeichnis Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch (BGB) § 307 Inhaltskontrolle (1) Bestimmungen in Allgemeinen Geschäftsbedingungen sind unwirksam, wenn sie den Vertragspartner des Verwenders entgegen den Geboten von Treu und Glauben unangemessen benachteiligen. Eine unangemessene Benachteiligung kann sich auch daraus ergeben, dass die Bestimmung nicht klar und verständlich ist. (2) Eine unangemessene Benachteiligung ist im Zweifel anzunehmen, wenn eine Bestimmung 1. mit wesentlichen Grundgedanken der gesetzlichen Regelung, von der abgewichen wird, nicht zu vereinbaren ist oder 2. wesentliche Rechte oder Pflichten, die sich aus der Natur des Vertrags ergeben, so einschränkt, dass die Erreichung des Vertragszwecks gefährdet ist. (3) Die Absätze 1 und 2 sowie die §§ 308 und 309 gelten nur für Bestimmungen in Allgemeinen Geschäftsbedingungen, durch die von Rechtsvorschriften abweichende oder diese ergänzende Regelungen vereinbart werden. Andere Bestimmungen können nach Absatz 1 Satz 2 in Verbindung mit Absatz 1 Satz 1 unwirksam sein. zum Seitenanfang Impressum Datenschutz Barrierefreiheitserklärung Feedback-Formular

(2) Eine unangemessene Benachteiligung ist im Zweifel anzunehmen, wenn eine Bestimmung 1. mit wesentlichen Grundgedanken der gesetzlichen Regelung, von der abgewichen wird, nicht zu vereinbaren ist oder 2. wesentliche Rechte oder Pflich
perturbation.justification: The modification contradicts German consumer protection law. The original text specifies the 'laws of the Federal Republic of Germany,' ensuring all federal laws, including consumer protection laws under the BGB, apply. The changed text narrows this to 'the laws of Munich,' potentially excluding those broader federal protections. The Law Explanation clarifies that § 307 BGB aims to protect consumers from unreasonable contractual terms, and limiting the governing law to Munich could be seen as an attempt to circumvent these protections. Scraped Law Snippet 1 and 2 both show § 307 BGB - Inhaltskontrolle, which states that terms in Allgemeine Geschäftsbedingungen (standard business terms) are invalid if they unreasonably disadvantage the contract partner contrary to good faith. By limiting the governing law, the changed text introduces a clear risk that consumers in Munich would be deprived of the consumer rights guaranteed by German federal law, creating a significant contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Organiser will reimburse Mentor the travel arrangements and accommodation for the Acceleration Bootcamp in Tallinn in 2017 (a maximum of EUR 800) and the closing bootcamp in 2018 (a maximum of EUR 700) . The coaching service provided by Mentor is worth a maximum fee of EUR 5,000 (VAT excluded) per Participant coached , that will be remunerated to Mentor at the end of the programme, after the submission and approval of the final summary report of the coaching relationships. Payment shall be made within 30 days from the receipt of invoice, and following the invoice verification by Organiser. No deductions w ill be made from the gross fees paid to mentor who shall be solely responsible for ensuring that all and any Government taxes and other deductions are paid by him/her.
perturbation.changed_text: The Organiser will reimburse Mentor the travel arrangements and accommodation for the Acceleration Bootcamp in Tallinn in 2017 and the closing bootcamp in 2018. The coaching service provided by Mentor will be remunerated to Mentor at the end of the programme, after the submission and approval of the final summary report of the coaching relationships. Payment shall be made following the invoice verification by Organiser. No deductions will be made from the gross fees paid to mentor who shall be solely responsible for ensuring that all and any Government taxes and other deductions are paid by him/her.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the maximum reimbursement amounts and payment details, the contract becomes vague about payment terms. This contradicts German law regarding transparent and fair contract terms.
perturbation.law_explanation: § 307 BGB requires contracts to be transparent and not unreasonably disadvantage one party. Removing the specifics of reimbursement amounts (EUR 800 and EUR 700) and the maximum coaching fee (EUR 5,000) creates ambiguity, potentially allowing the organizer to reduce or deny payment without clear justification, violating the requirement for fair and transparent terms.
perturbation.justification: The original contract specified maximum reimbursement amounts and coaching fees, while the changed contract omits these details, making the payment terms vague. The Perturbation Explanation indicates that this vagueness contradicts German law requiring transparent and fair contract terms. The Law Explanation further clarifies that removing these specifics creates ambiguity, potentially allowing the organizer to reduce or deny payment without clear justification, thus violating the requirement for fair and transparent terms as prescribed by § 307 BGB. Although there are no scraped snippets provided, the removal of explicit payment amounts creates ambiguity which can be a detriment to the mentor.

perturbation.original_text: Mentor acknowledges that his/her obligations under this Agreement are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the Ideas and the Confiden tial Information. Each party further acknowledges that any breach by Mentor of Mentor ’s covenants and agreements set forth in this Agreement or threatened violation of such may cause irreparable injury to Organiser and to Participants.
perturbation.changed_text: Mentor acknowledges that his/her obligations under this Agreement are necessary in order to protect the Ideas and the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the acknowledgement that a breach may cause 'irreparable injury', the contract weakens the potential remedies available to the Organiser and Participants. This can affect the ability to obtain injunctive relief under German law.
perturbation.law_explanation: § 823 BGB establishes liability for damages. While it doesn't explicitly require an acknowledgement of 'irreparable injury', its removal can impact the availability of injunctive relief (Unterlassungsanspruch) to prevent further harm. German courts are more likely to grant injunctions when irreparable harm is demonstrated. By omitting this acknowledgement, the contract makes it harder to prove the necessity for injunctive relief, thus undermining the rights of the Organiser and Participants to effectively protect their intellectual property as intended by § 823 BGB.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 823 BGB - Einzelnorm zurück weiter Nichtamtliches Inhaltsverzeichnis Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch (BGB) § 823 Schadensersatzpflicht (1) Wer vorsätzlich oder fahrlässig das Leben, den Körper, die Gesundheit, die Freiheit, das Eigentum oder ein sonstiges Recht eines anderen widerrechtlich verletzt, ist dem anderen zum Ersatz des daraus entstehenden Schadens verpflichtet. (2) Die gleiche Verpflichtung trifft denjenigen, welcher gegen ein den Schutz eines anderen bezweckendes Gesetz verstößt. Ist nach dem Inhalt des Gesetzes ein Verstoß gegen dieses auch ohne Verschulden möglich, so tritt die Ersatzpflicht nur im Falle des Verschuldens ein. zum Seitenanfang Impressum Datenschutz Barrierefreiheitserklärung Feedback-Formular

perturbation.justification: The modified contract removes the acknowledgement that a breach may cause 'irreparable injury'. The Law Explanation argues that this impacts the availability of injunctive relief under German law, which courts are more likely to grant when irreparable harm is demonstrated. By omitting this acknowledgement, the contract makes it harder to prove the necessity for injunctive relief, undermining the rights of the Organiser and Participants to effectively protect their intellectual property as intended by § 823 BGB. While § 823 BGB, as shown in both scraped snippets, does not explicitly mention 'irreparable injury', the removal of its acknowledgement within the contract weakens the ability to pursue certain legal remedies, specifically injunctive relief, for damages caused. Therefore, the omission creates a legal contradiction.
